title: Unit A-1 Self-Check (3 questions, for confirming understanding -- no pass/fail record is saved)

questions.q: Which of the following correctly describes something generative AI does NOT do when producing text?
choices: Construct text based on statistical patterns acquired during training
Probabilistically predict the next word based on the immediately preceding context
Always directly access an external website to fact-check, every time it responds
Reflect tendencies in the variety of writing styles it encountered during training
questions.explanation: What generative AI actually does when producing text is construct text based on statistical patterns acquired during training (option 1), probabilistically predict the next word based on the immediately preceding context (option 2), and reflect tendencies in the variety of writing styles it encountered during training (option 4). It does not, however, always directly access an external website to fact-check every time it responds (with the exception of some tools that incorporate search functionality). Note that 'acquiring patterns' itself happens during the prior training phase, not at the moment the text is produced.

questions.q: A medical student said, "Generative AI is conscious and thinks for itself to choose its answers." Which of the following is the most appropriate response to this statement?
choices: That is correct -- AI thinks autonomously and makes deliberate decisions with genuine intent, exactly like a human being does
AI has no consciousness or understanding; it merely calculates next-word probabilities from patterns it has learned
AI has no consciousness, but it does possess genuine internal emotions such as joy, anxiety, and empathy
AI's "thinking" is always more accurate than that of a human physician, regardless of specialty or experience
questions.explanation: Current generative AI does not possess consciousness or understanding; it merely calculates the probability of the next word from the vast amount of text it has learned and generates output based on that calculation. Claims that it 'thinks autonomously and makes deliberate decisions with genuine intent' (option 1), that it 'possesses genuine internal emotions' (option 3), or that it is 'always more accurate than a physician, regardless of specialty' (option 4) are all mistaken forms of anthropomorphizing or overestimating AI.

questions.q: Which of the following, framed in terms of how LLMs work, best explains why it is particularly dangerous to use generative AI output as-is without checking it?
choices: AI always automatically reflects every revision to medical guidelines and package inserts on the very day they are published, so no checking is necessary
AI is not a mechanism that guarantees factual accuracy -- it generates a plausible-sounding sequence of words -- so incorrect information can be output as natural-sounding text
The content of AI output is always guaranteed to be correct under the terms of service and relevant law, so no checking is necessary
Whenever AI output contains an error, it is always designed to display a warning at that spot, so no checking is necessary
questions.explanation: Generative AI is not a mechanism for verifying factual accuracy; it probabilistically generates plausible-sounding text based on its training data. As a result, even incorrect content can be output as natural, persuasive-sounding text, which is why checking the content (fact-checking) is indispensable. The claim that it always automatically reflects every guideline revision the day it is published (option 1), that correctness is always guaranteed by the terms of service and the law (option 3), and that an error always triggers a warning (option 4) are all built on the same mistaken premise that correctness is automatically guaranteed.
